amendments.normalized_body: The `classify_exit_tests` module in `src/adapters/process_backend.rs:2954-3005` uses `std::os::unix::process::ExitStatusExt` unconditionally (no `#[cfg(unix)]` gate on the module). This will fail to compile on non-Unix platforms. Add `#[cfg(unix)]` to the `classify_exit_tests` module to match the conditional compilation pattern used elsewhere in the file (e.g. the `#[cfg(unix)]` block inside `classify_exit_failure` itself).

In `src/contexts/workflow_composition/engine.rs` backoff path (~line 4634), after `preserve_interrupted_run(snapshot)` sets `snapshot.interrupted_run = snapshot.active_run.clone()`, the code then sets `snapshot.active_run = None`. Later on successful snapshot write, it restores via `snapshot.active_run = snapshot.interrupted_run.take()`. However, if the backoff sleep completes but the cancellation check at line 4714 fires, `fail_run_result` is called — but `snapshot.active_run` has already been restored (line 4711), while `snapshot.interrupted_run` was consumed by `.take()`. Verify that `fail_run_result` → `fail_run` → `preserve_interrupted_run` handles the case where `interrupted_run` is `None` (since it was already taken). The `preserve_interrupted_run` call inside `fail_run` will overwrite `interrupted_run` with the current `active_run`, so this appears safe. No change needed if confirmed.

`src/adapters/process_backend.rs:985-992`, `src/adapters/process_backend.rs:1062-1088`, `src/adapters/process_backend.rs:412-419`, and `src/adapters/process_backend.rs:1530-1536` still only kill/reap the direct child PID. The command is spawned without its own process group/job object, and the timeout path only calls `send_sigkill()`/`Child::kill()` on that one child before declaring teardown confirmed. If the backend shells out or backgrounds a helper that inherits stdout/stderr, the parent can exit while the descendant keeps running; the invocation is reported as timed out/retryable, but the leaked descendant survives into the next retry. That misses the acceptance criterion that hanging backend processes are killed after timeout.

`src/adapters/process_backend.rs:1064-1088` drops the invocation from `active_children` and returns immediately when timeout teardown is not confirmed, but unlike `cancel()` there is no background reap fallback. The existing cancel path explicitly keeps reaping in the background at `src/adapters/process_backend.rs:1380-1390` via `spawn_background_reap()` (`src/adapters/process_backend.rs:1520-1525`); the timeout path does not. A slow-to-die child can therefore outlive tracking and subsequent retries/cancellations will no longer be able to find it.

Timeout-like failures that come back as `TransportFailure` lose their diagnostics. The timeout branch intentionally downgrades unconfirmed teardown to `TransportFailure` at `src/adapters/process_backend.rs:1068-1075`, but `invoke()` only preserves failed artifacts when `error.failure_class() == Timeout` and otherwise calls `prepared.cleanup()` at `src/adapters/process_backend.rs:1259-1266` (same pattern in the stale-session retry path at `src/adapters/process_backend.rs:1298-1308`). That deletes the schema/last-message temp files for the hardest timeout failures to debug.

`src/adapters/process_backend.rs:1064-1088` drops the invocation from `active_children` and returns immediately when timeout teardown is not confirmed, but unlike `cancel()` there is no background reap fallback. Timeout-like failures that come back as `TransportFailure` lose their diagnostics. payload.summary: Final review accepted 4 amendment(s); restart required.
